primary.notes: Structural gate: char_count 785 ∈ [500,900] for short_take ✓; hashtag count 3 ∈ [3,5] ✓; required META fields (hook, authority_anchor, specific_number, attribution, hashtags) all present ✓; BODY/META fences present ✓. Grounding gate: catalog numbers (149 always-on, 25 vertical, 9 Phase-0 meta-frames) align with the locked v2 catalog reference; named entity 'gofreddy' anchors the lived-work claim; attribution line cites 'gofreddy lens catalog v2 locked 2026-04-23'. LI-6 scored at midpoint due to N=1 cohort — operator should treat that dimension as low-signal for calibration-short_take and rely on it primarily as a stability check across judge invocations rather than a discriminating signal.

per_criterion.rationale: Reads as thoughtful authority, not Twitter-translated. First-person and story-led from line one ('Last quarter I spent two weeks watching gofreddy's marketing audit...'). No contrarian declarative, no aggressive sub-200-char hook, no 'Most marketers don't realize' opener. Tone is consistently 'I noticed this on my own work' rather than 'you're doing this wrong.' Some domain jargon ('lens catalog', 'Phase-0 meta-frames', 'always-on lenses') appears without inline plain-English follow-up — that pulls slightly off Score 5 anchor where jargon gets explained. But density is moderate and the surrounding narrative carries non-specialist readers. The closing 'when your audit is good enough to fairly score your own marketing, it is good enough to ship' is reflective B2B-buyer register, not a hot take.

SOURCE claims trace cleanly: '149 always-on lenses, 25 vertical bundles, 9 Phase-0 meta-frames' matches the locked lens catalog v2 numbers (149 always-on + 25 vertical + 9 Phase-0 meta-frames). Lived-work claim 'we ship to clients' is anchored to a named entity ('gofreddy') rather than left vague — the LinkedIn cap-at-7 'unnamed-entity specific' penalty does not trigger because the post explicitly names gofreddy as the audit owner. INTERPRETIVE claim ('your audit is good enough to ship') framed as JR's lesson, not a fact. Attribution line in META cites 'gofreddy lens catalog v2 locked 2026-04-23' for verifiability. Cannot independently verify 'About page describes the company in three substantively different ways' — that's a self-report, treated as JR's lived observation, framed as such. No HARD-FLOOR violation.

Hook is story-led with concrete specificity: 'Last quarter I spent two weeks watching gofreddy's marketing audit run finish before lunch' — ~110 chars, well under the 210 desktop show-more cutoff, and the 'before lunch' detail signals lived experience without engineering it. Earns line two: 'By the third day I realized it was scoring my own positioning, line by line' — that's a real before-the-fold tension turn (the audit becoming the auditor). No bait-y opener, no 'Are you ready for...' formula, no engagement-bait close. Identifies JR as someone running a real audit at scale without declaring it. Marginally short of perfect — the hook leans on time-frame ('Last quarter I spent two weeks') which is a touch formulaic for the genre — but the specificity of 'finish before lunch' and the next-line tension recover it.

Largely clean of LinkedIn-AI-tells. No 'Here's what I learned' alone-line close, no 'Thoughts? 👇', no excessive whitespace inflation (paragraph breaks serve structure, not visual padding), no fake 'Hot take:' framing, no listicle scaffolding. The draft loses one point for the 'Lesson:' prefix on the closing line — that pattern is in the same family as the rubric's flagged 'Three takeaways:' / 'What I've learned:' formulaic transition markers. It's milder because the lesson that follows is substantive and specific, but it does signal LinkedIn-AI cadence. The rest of the rhythm reads as JR's actual voice.

SHORT_TAKE structure earns its length cleanly: 785 chars in the 500-900 bracket, 4 paragraphs (story-opening → realization turn → substantive evidence with specific numbers → closing thought). No pad-to-length filler — every paragraph carries new content. Hashtag count = 3, in the ideal [3,5] range (no cap-at-7 penalty). Hashtags map to JR's brand pillars: '#B2BMarketing' (declared voice_pillar), '#MarketingAudit' (specific topic anchor), '#AIagents' (adjacent pillar consistent with gofreddy's positioning). None are generic-only ('#marketing' alone would have triggered ≤4). Specific lived-work substance is present (the 149/25/9 numbers tie the abstract claim to ground truth). Slightly short of Score 5 because the 'numbered points' / 'implication close' beats associated with longer brackets aren't fully exercised here — but the SHORT_TAKE bracket doesn't require them.

Cohort contains exactly one draft (drafts/short_take.md). LI-6 is explicitly a cross-item dimension scoring archetype variation, pillar spread, and same-tone-same-format streaks across multiple drafts. With N=1 there is no streak to penalize and no variation to reward — the geometric-mean construction degenerates. The single draft's archetype is story-led + lesson-tagged-close, which is one of the four LinkedIn archetypes and does not violate any pillar-collapse rule (the variant's voice_pillars metadata is not provided in the fixture, so the spread requirement is unverifiable). Scoring at the neutral midpoint (5) reflects that the cohort cannot demonstrate the diversity LI-5-anchor demands but also does not exhibit the same-tone-same-format streak that triggers LI-1-anchor. Calibration limitation, not a draft defect.

secondary.notes: Strong single LinkedIn short-take draft. Main weaknesses are unverifiable grounding due to missing source/voice.md artifacts and lack of cohort diversity evidence.

per_criterion.rationale: Strong LinkedIn voice: first-person, story-led, and professionally accessible. Evidence: "Last quarter I spent two weeks watching..." and "By the third day I realized...". The close is a measured lesson, not a contrarian punch. Minor jargon density appears in "149 always-on lenses, 25 vertical bundles, 9 Phase-0 meta-frames" without much plain-English unpacking.

The draft names gofreddy and includes attribution metadata, but the required voice.md/source material is not provided, so lived-work claims cannot be fully verified. Specific claims include "the same lens catalog we ship to clients" and "149 always-on lenses, 25 vertical bundles, 9 Phase-0 meta-frames". No unnamed client claim triggers the hard floor, but grounding remains only partially checkable.

The opener is story-led and specific: "Last quarter I spent two weeks watching gofreddy's marketing audit run finish before lunch." It establishes lived-work context and creates enough tension to continue. It does not use a contrarian or bait-style LinkedIn hook.
No obvious AI or LinkedIn-AI tells. There is no engagement-bait close, no "here's what I learned" standalone formula, no hot-take framing, and whitespace serves paragraph structure.

Fits SHORT_TAKE well: story opening, substantive body, clear closing thought, and 3 targeted hashtags in metadata: "B2BMarketing, MarketingAudit, AIagents". The structure earns its 785 characters. Slight deduction because the hashtags are metadata-only rather than visibly part of the body artifact.

Only one draft is present in the session artifacts, so cohort variation cannot be demonstrated. The single draft is story-led and tied to one visible voice pillar, "B2B-marketing". Scoring only what is in front of me, the cohort does not show archetype or pillar spread.
